title: Doraemon: Nobita no E Sekai Monogatari
titleRomaji: Doraemon: Nobita no E Sekai Monogatari
titleNative: 映画ドラえもん のび太の絵世界物語
synonyms: Doraemon: The Tale of Nobita's Picture World
synopsis: Doraemon, Nobita, and friends enter a painting into a medieval European world. In the painting, they meet Claire and Milo, children from the country of Artoria. They also encounter a small demon with wings named Chai. Together, they face a powerful enemy for a legendary jewel. (Source: Anime News Network)

title: Star Catch! Teenieping
titleNative: 슈팅스타 캐치! 티니핑
synopsis: A regular day begins until a mysterious Black Hole has appeared towards the Space Star Village, sucking every Star Teenieping and the planets in! This brings off a new adventure with Romi and Heartsping on Harmony Town with 3 Royal Teeniepings to help them out! Will they be able to catch all the Star Teeniepings and restore peace?

synopsis: Kakuei Kakkun Tanaka is a superhero who hangs out with his friends. In his misadventures, he becomes prime minister of Japan, visits New York and prevents a nuclear war. (Source: The Anime Encyclopedia)

title: Dokuhaku Suru Universal Yoko Mercator Egg Man
titleRomaji: Dokuhaku Suru Universal Yoko Mercator Egg Man
titleNative: 独白するユニバーサル横メルカトル Egg Man
synonyms: Monologues of Universal Transverse Mercator Egg Man
synopsis: A psychopathic killer known as Egg Man has been caught, incarcerated and is awaiting his death sentence. Karen, a beautiful and brave police woman, is assigned to the "Egg Man" case to find the whereabouts of a missing dead body. Egg Man meets his cellmate, Prisoner 205. Who is Prisoner 205? Is Prisoner 205 another in-mate awaiting his death sentence as well or an android utilized by the police to collect information about him and his knowledge of the corpse? Who is really manipulating whom? (Source: Anime News Network)

title: KIZUMONOGATARI -Koyomi Vamp-
titleNative: 傷物語 -こよみヴァンプ-
synopsis: Compilation film of the three Kizumonogatari movies. Spring break, an interval of time between the second and third year of high school. It was one fateful evening when Koyomi Araragi encounters her. Kiss-shot Acerola-orion Heart-under-blade. Legendary Vampire. Killer of Apparitions. King of Apparitions. Her legs and arms were cruelly torn apart, her extravagant dress soaked in red; she's a monster who finds herself in a tragic state, laying in a pool of her own blood. However, Koyomi finds her to be beautiful this way. On the brink of death after losing her four limbs, she asks Koyomi to save her. After experiencing fear, chaos, and finally conflict, Koyomi gives his own blood in order to save her. But when he awakens, he finds himself re-born as her vampire kin. Koyomi wishes to revert to being a human from a vampire. However, he must take back all of Kiss-shot's limbs to do so. Working as a negotiator, Meme Oshino stands in middle ground as Koyomi attempts to recapture her four limb

titleNative: おらぁグズラだど

title: Sanpou Shoujo
titleNative: 算法少女
synopsis: An adaptation of a children's novel of the same name published in 1973. Endo Hiroko is the author, who was inspired to write the story from a tale her father told her as a child. It follows a 13 year old girl named Aki. During a pilgrimage to Sensou-ji (a Buddhist temple located in Asakusa, Tokyo), she found a mistake in the sangaku. News spread quickly of her intellect and eventually landed upon Arima Yoriyuki, the lord of the Kurume Domain (he himself was famous for finding the rational approximation of pi). He hired Aki to become an arithmetic teacher for the region's princess, but hired others as well. In a math competition, Aki fought against other geniuses and prodigies, all for the hopes of earning a wage so that she may one day open a school for poor children so that she can teach them arithmetic as well.

titleRomaji: Saitama Bousou Saizensen Flag! Shinimonogurui no Seishun!!
titleNative: 埼玉暴走最前線 フラッグ! 死にものぐるいの青春!!
synopsis: Based on the manga Flag! by Hotta Akio, which was serialized in Young King from 1992 to 1995. The OVA depicts the tumultuous daily lives of three boys, including Noboru, Akira and Haruo, who inherit the flag of the locally famous fugitive tribe "Moonlight".
